pr.title: Bump `dtk` to v0.7.3 and run `dol apply`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the repository's configured decomp toolkit tag from dtk v0.7.2 to v0.7.3 and regenerated GALE01 symbol metadata with `dol apply`. The resulting churn was concentrated in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, where many local auto-generated symbol suffixes and anonymous labels were renumbered while addresses, sections, sizes, and scopes in the shown excerpts remained tied to the same locations.

key_files.reason: Changed the dtk tool tag from `v0.7.2` to `v0.7.3`.

Regenerated by `dol apply`; 481 additions and 481 deletions across 76 hunks, mostly renumbering local symbols such as `@230` to `@235` and `$` suffixed locals such as `vec0_init$494` to `vec0_init$499`.

what_changed: `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was refreshed after the tool bump.

The diff excerpt shows systematic local-symbol renumbering in `.rodata` and `.data`, including anonymous labels like `@465` to `@470`, string labels like `@230` to `@235`, and local suffixed names like `shadowBallSFX$382` to `shadowBallSFX$387`.

Named global or semi-semantic symbols visible around the churn, such as `ftLk_Init_Vec3Group`, `CostumeListsForeachCharacter`, `ftFx_Init_AnimDatFilename`, and `ftCl_Init_MotionStateTable`, were not shown being semantically renamed.
The PR had no body text, issue comments, review comments, or recorded reviews in the provided slice.
smart_moves: Kept the tool-version bump and the generated `dol apply` output together, making the symbol-file churn traceable to the dtk upgrade rather than to hand edits.
Limited changed files to the tooling configuration and generated symbol metadata, avoiding unrelated source or matching changes.
Accepted mechanical renumbering in the generated symbols file rather than trying to manually preserve unstable anonymous/local compiler-style labels.
decomp_lessons: When bumping dtk, run the project regeneration step (`dol apply`) and commit the resulting symbol metadata changes so local checkouts and CI use the same tool assumptions.

Anonymous symbols of the form `@NNN` and local suffixed names like `name$NNN` should be treated as generated, unstable identifiers; future work should avoid depending on their numeric suffixes as semantic evidence.
For generated symbol churn, reviewers should inspect whether addresses, sections, sizes, scopes, and data annotations remain coherent, rather than focusing only on the changed local label numbers.
A large balanced add/delete count in `symbols.txt` can be benign if it is caused by a tool regeneration and mostly preserves address mappings.
naming_conventions: Generated anonymous locals use `@<number>` names, for example `@230`, `@235`, `@470`, and `@540`.
Some local generated symbols combine a base name with a numeric suffix using `$`, for example `vec0_init$499`, `shadowBallSFX$387`, and `it_803F5B60$175`.
Address-derived fallback names remain in the pattern `<module>_<address>`, for example `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0`, `it_803B86BC`, and `grSt_803E274C`.
More meaningful global data names use subsystem prefixes and descriptive suffixes, for example `ftFx_Init_AnimDatFilename`, `ftPp_Init_CostumeStrings`, and `ftCl_Init_MotionStateTable`.
The diff supports treating numeric suffix changes on locals as tooling artifacts, not intentional semantic renames.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No function matching or assembly rewrite tactic is evidenced in this PR.
The relevant tactic was metadata regeneration: bump `dtk`, run `dol apply`, and review the produced `symbols.txt` churn.
For future matching work, prefer stable named globals and address/type metadata over generated local labels whose numbering may change between dtk versions.

review_feedback.detail: The provided PR slice contains zero issue comments, zero review comments, and zero reviews, so no reviewer guidance can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find PRs that bump dtk and run dol apply
Find symbol regeneration PRs with many @NNN local label renames
Find guidance on treating generated symbols.txt local suffixes as unstable
